channel.title: The Grant Desk - Research & Journalism, EU, next 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Research & Journalism, EU, next 30 days.

item.title: European Commission: Safeguarding Europe's Born-Digital Heritage (PPPA-2026-BORN-DIGITAL)
item.description:
Organisation: European Commission (Pilot Projects and Preparatory Actions, Culture strand)

Location: European Union. Consortium of at least 5 independent entities from 5 different EU member states required.

Award: Up to €1,985,000, covering up to 85% of eligible project costs (15% co-financing required). No application fee.

Duration: Project duration to be defined in the proposal; covers a scoping and mapping phase across EU member states.

Deadline: 16 Jul 2026

The European Commission is offering a single grant of up to €1,985,000 (85% co-financing) for a project focused on safeguarding born-digital cultural heritage across EU member states. Scope includes: assessing the preservation state of born-digital art, web archives, video games, and digital content created outside cultural institutions; mapping EU and national legal frameworks; identifying non-legislative barriers (technical, financial, organisational, cultural); and providing guidance on creating preservation copies of licensed or legally accessible materials. This is a scoping and advocacy project, not a digitisation grant. ELIGIBILITY: the application must be led by a consortium of at least 5 independent entities from 5 different EU countries, which rules out solo applicants and single-country organisations. Grant reference: PPPA-2026-BORN-DIGITAL. Deadline: 16 July 2026. Verify the current deadline at ec.europa.eu before applying (one secondary source listed 1 June 2026; the primary call-fiche PDF and the Cyprus government funding portal both confirm 16 July 2026).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: French Institutes for Advanced Study (FIAS) Fellowship Programme 2027-2028
item.description:
Organisation: French Institutes for Advanced Study (FIAS) network: Aix-Marseille, Cergy, Loire Valley (Orleans-Tours), Lyon, Montpellier, Nantes, Paris

Location: Seven Institutes for Advanced Study across France (Aix-Marseille, Cergy, Loire Valley/Orleans-Tours, Lyon, Montpellier, Nantes, Paris)

Award: EUR 2,200/month living allowance; social security coverage; accommodation; research and training budget; travel expenses covered. No application fee.

Duration: 10-month fellowship during the 2027/2028 academic year

Deadline: 25 Jun 2026

FIAS Fellowship Programme offers 10-month fellowships at seven Institutes for Advanced Study in France, welcoming high-level international scholars and scientists to develop innovative research projects. 29 POSITIONS for 2027/2028: Aix-Marseille (7); Cergy (3); Loire Valley/Orleans-Tours (2); Lyon (3); Montpellier (2); Nantes (4); Paris (8). DISCIPLINES: all Social Sciences and Humanities (SSH) plus all other research fields interfacing with the SSH. ELIGIBILITY: outstanding researchers at all career levels, from postdoctoral to senior scientists. Minimum: PhD plus 2 years of research experience at the time of application. Researchers from all countries are eligible BUT must have spent no more than 12 months in France during the three years prior to the application deadline. COMMON STANDARDS across all FIAS: living allowance of EUR 2,200/month; social security coverage; accommodation; research and training budget; travel expenses covered. DEADLINE: 25 June 2026. APPLY via the FIAS-FP call for applications page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CAIS Research Sabbaticals (Fellowships) 2027-28
item.description:
Organisation: Center for Advanced Internet Studies (CAIS), Bochum, Germany

Location: Bochum, Germany (residential at CAIS; rent-free, fully furnished apartment provided plus private office)

Award: Sabbatical leave on full salary: additional €600/month grant. No regular income: €2,000/month grant. Regular income below €1,400/month: top-up to the full grant. Alternative: reimbursement of salary or substitute costs within reasonable limits. €100/month extra per child under 18 for fellows on a full or compensatory grant. CAIS also covers one return trip to Bochum (or a daily commute if local), provides a rent-free fully furnished apartment and a private office. Fellows can request financial support for research expenses, invite a Visiting Fellow for up to 3 weeks of collaboration, and invite up to three European experts for half-day workshops (CAIS covers travel, accommodation and a daily allowance of up to €24).

Duration: 3 or 6 months. 6-month fellowships usually start in October or April; 3-month fellowships start in October, January, April or July. Exceptions possible by arrangement.

Deadline: 23 Jul 2026

Residential research sabbaticals at CAIS, explicitly open to excellent scholars AND practitioners across all career stages and disciplines (not academia-only). Funds individual projects on the societal impact of digital transformation, including pure research and applied projects that develop new theories, methods or perspectives for practice. Project must be self-contained with specific milestones and produce an independent output suitable for short-timeframe publication: peer-reviewed paper or conference contribution, book chapter, policy paper, or prototype. Fellows join a vibrant interdisciplinary research community with regular joint activities (breakfast Tuesdays, colloquium and dinner Wednesdays, occasional workshops Thursdays) and an international network of alumni, working groups and affiliates. In Germany, full and compensatory grants are not subject to social security contributions and are usually tax-exempt; fellows resident abroad should verify their own tax position. Note: CAIS is currently reviewing the application format and selection process for the next call, so details may change. Next call publishes at the beginning of June 2026; deadline 23 July 2026 for fellowships in the period October 2027 to March 2028. Contact: Dr. Esther Laufer, esther.laufer@cais-research.de.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
